CVE-2026-26030 targets Microsoft Semantic Kernel Python SDK with a CVSS 10.0 remote code execution flaw. The InMemoryVectorStore filter turns a prompt injection into arbitrary code execution. Patch is v1.39.4.  The attack works through InMemoryCollection._parse_and_validate_filter. A crafted filter_str accesses Python object internals like __globals__, then escalates to full code execution. This is CWE-94 code injection, not a prompt safety issue.  This rewrites the threat model for AI agents. Prompt injection was a content integrity problem. Now it is a code execution vector. Any agent pipeline using Semantic Kernel for RAG is exposed if unpatched. The vector database your agent queries can own the box.  What to do: upgrade Semantic Kernel Python SDK to v1.39.4 or later. If you cannot upgrade today, stop using InMemoryVectorStore in production immediately. There is no workaround that keeps it safe.  This is why AgentVet exists. We vet MCP servers, skills, and agent pipelines before they hit production. One unpatched dependency is all it takes. Review your stack → agentvet.io/skills

🚨 Installing dependencies has started to feel unsafe.  Every week it’s: • typosquat malware • poisoned packages • postinstall persistence attacks • another critical dependency CVE  So I built depsec — a CLI tool that sits between you and your package manager and scans packages BEFORE they ever touch node_modules. (npm support live now — pip & cargo coming soon)  depsec: → validates package names → detects typosquats & homoglyphs → resolves the full dependency tree → quarantines tarballs → scans install scripts & persistence paths → verifies checksums → only then allows install  Not trying to replace npm or build a giant security platform.  I just wanted something that reduces the anxiety of installing dependencies in modern dev workflows — especially with AI tools hallucinating fake package names nonstop.  Built after seeing incidents like: • Mini Shai-Hulud • Next.js ecosystem compromises • TanStack/npm supply-chain scares  Still prerelease, but early access is opening soon. Long before ransomware gangs, spyware, and crypto miners existed, the world’s first widely recognized malware was something surprisingly simple.  It was called “Creeper.”  Back in the early 1970s, computers were huge machines connected through an early network called ARPANET, the precursor to the modern internet. A programmer named Bob Thomas created an experimental self-replicating program that could move from one system to another.  It wasn’t designed to steal data. It wasn’t destructive. It was basically a proof of concept.  When Creeper infected a machine, it displayed a message:  “I’m the creeper, catch me if you can!”  That was it 😭  But the important part was not the message. It was the idea.  For the first time, the world saw software capable of spreading itself automatically between computers without physical transfer. That single concept would later evolve into worms, viruses, botnets, ransomware, and modern cyber warfare.  Ironically, the solution to Creeper became the world’s first antivirus program.  A second program called “Reaper” was created specifically to hunt Creeper down and remove it from infected systems.  So the cybersecurity industry basically began with: one engineer making malware for fun… and another engineer immediately building a tool to kill it.  And 50+ years later, we’re still repeating the same cycle on a much larger scale.

I am pleased to share with my friends, colleagues and professional network the arrival of my official Master of Science degree Certificate in Computer Science from Imo State University, Owerri - IMSU  The programme concentration was on Data Communication and Network Engineering, and completed at Distinction level.  This milestone means a lot to me because the journey was not without challenges. There were moments that tested my strength, discipline and resolve. Yet, by God’s sheer grace, I surmounted the hurdles and completed the programme successfully.  Some may wonder why I chose to pursue another master’s degree, having already acquired four master’s degrees in different areas: Cloud Computing, Digital Transformation, Cybersecurity and Digital Forensics.  The answer is simple. Technology does not stand still. Each field connects to the other, but each also demands depth.  Cloud Computing gave me a stronger understanding of modern infrastructure and scalable digital services. Digital Transformation helped me understand how technology drives organisational change and business value. Cybersecurity strengthened my capacity to protect digital systems and manage risk. Digital Forensics deepened my knowledge of investigation, evidence and incident response.  Data Communication and Network Engineering completes an important part of that knowledge chain.  No digital system can function effectively without reliable networks. Cloud platforms, cybersecurity operations, digital services, enterprise systems, smart infrastructure and modern business processes all depend on secure, resilient and well-engineered communication networks.  I chose to further in this area because I wanted stronger technical depth in the foundation that supports today’s digital economy. For me, continuous professional development is not about collecting degrees. It is about building competence, staying relevant and preparing to solve complex technology problems with clarity and confidence.  I give all glory to God for His grace and strength. I also appreciate my family, mentors, lecturers, colleagues and everyone who supported me on this journey.  This achievement is both a testimony and a fresh motivation to keep learning, keep growing and keep contributing to the technology profession and society.  Citp' Destiny Young, PhD (Cand.), FIIM, MCPN, MNCS, MNIN, MCIA, MIAENG, Rotary International Cybersecurity Fellow

Fakta yang bikin gw kaget:  Di Web3, bug hunter terbaik = developer terbaik.  Lo gak bisa nemuin yang rusak kalau lo gak paham gimana seharusnya jalan.  Build dulu. ERC-20 token. Voting system. sample_tweets.text: 🚨A recent post on an underground data-sharing forum alleges the unauthorized exposure of a comprehensive database belonging to the Israel Defense Forces (IDF). According to the post’s title, the leak reportedly contains highly sensitive personal and operational data, including full names, phone numbers, residential addresses, weapon specifications, and military service records.  While the forum entry itself lacks sample files or detailed documentation, the scope of the alleged breach raises significant cybersecurity and national security concerns. Military databases of this nature are typically protected under strict defense frameworks, and the exposure of active and reserve personnel information could compromise individual safety, operational security, and tactical readiness.  The IDF serves as Israel’s unified national military, comprising approximately 170,000 active-duty personnel and over 460,000 reservists. As a critical defense organization, the integrity of its internal systems is paramount to national security. The incident falls under the Defense/Military Government sector, with the country of operation identified as Israel.  #Israel #Defense #Forces #IDF #Database #Darkweb #ThreatIntelligence

🚨A threat actor operating under the alias 'mosad' is offering classified US military documents for sale on underground forums, including FY-2027 posture statements from multiple US combatant commands. Activity on darkweb platforms reveals the actor is marketing sensitive defense materials through Telegram, Session, Tox, and Matrix channels.  The leaked documents include AFRICOM's FY-27 posture statement, CENTCOM's FY-27 posture statement, and combined Navy-Marine Corps FY-27 posture statements. Additional materials listed for sale include a Government Accountability Office report on Air Force depot maintenance and Air Force testimony covering bombers, tankers, and cargo aircraft. The actor claims access to 'TOP SECRET' reports and states they are 'not limited to USA' in their operations.  The threat actor has established a presence across multiple encrypted messaging platforms, operating Telegram channels including t.me/InsideMossad and t.me/dazer. They advertise sample documents and pricing information, with escrow services available for transactions. Previous activity attributed to 'mosad' has involved claims of exfiltrating classified documents from military research institutes and intelligence organizations, including alleged compromises of Chinese PLA facilities and DARPA-related technical reports.  US Africa Command and US Central Command posture statements are delivered annually to Congress through the Senate Armed Services Committee and represent critical assessments of US military strategy in their respective regions. AFRICOM covers US military operations across Africa, while CENTCOM oversees US military interests in the Middle East, Central Asia, and parts of South Asia. These documents typically contain sensitive operational planning, threat assessments, and resource allocation strategies.  The sale of such documents represents a significant national security concern, as posture statements inform congressional oversight of military spending and operational readiness. The actor's claims of access to classified defense materials across multiple US military branches suggest either a high-level insider threat or successful penetration of secure government networks. The US Department of Defense and relevant intelligence agencies would likely be investigating the source of the leak and the identity of the threat actor.

제공해주신 위협 인텔리전스 데이터를 분석한 결과입니다.

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 AI를 활용하여 취약점 탐색 및 익스플로잇 코드 생성을 가속화하고 있으며, 개발자 워크스테이션을 소프트웨어 공급망 공격의 핵심 타겟으로 삼고 있습니다. 특히 `Ransomware` 그룹은 데이터 암호화에서 데이터 유출(Data Extortion) 위주로 전략을 전환하고 있으며, `Zero-day` 취약점을 활용한 권한 상승 공격이 빈번하게 발생하고 있습니다. 또한, `SaaS` 플랫폼 및 `Cloud` 환경의 설정 오류를 악용한 공격이 증가하고 있어 보안 팀의 대응 자동화와 `Patching` 주기의 단축이 시급한 상황입니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **ShinyHunters**: `7-Eleven` 및 `Canvas` 플랫폼을 대상으로 대규모 데이터 유출 및 랜섬웨어 협박 수행.
- **UNC6671**: `BlackFile` 캠페인을 통해 `Vishing`과 결합된 금전 갈취 활동 전개.
- **Kimsuky**: `PebbleDash` 및 `AppleSeed` 악성코드를 활용하여 조직 대상 사이버 간첩 활동 수행.
- **FrostyNeighbor**: 지속적인 사이버 간첩 활동을 위해 악성코드 체인 업데이트 및 공격 범위 확장.
- **UAT-8616**: `Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN` 취약점(`CVE-2026-20127`, `CVE-2026-20182`)을 악용하여 공격 수행.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **정부 사업**: SGA솔루션즈가 AI 기반 한국형 제로트러스트 플랫폼 사업 수주.
- **피싱 공격**: 국세청 및 금융기관을 사칭한 `PhaaS`(Phishing-as-a-Service) 기반 기업 타겟 피싱 메일 유포.
- **악성 확장 프로그램**: `TikTok` 다운로더로 위장한 악성 크롬 확장 프로그램 유포.
- **정책**: 한국 정부의 딥페이크 규제 법안 마련 및 선거 관련 보안 대응 테스트베드 운영.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점 (CVSS 우선)
- **CVE-2026-26030**: Microsoft Semantic Kernel Python SDK, RCE, CVSS 10.0, 악용 여부 확인 필요.
- **CVE-2026-41940**: cPanel, RCE, CVSS 9.8, `Sorry` 랜섬웨어에 의해 활발히 악용 중.
- **CVE-2026-42945**: NGINX, Heap Buffer Overflow, CVSS 9.2, 야생에서 활발히 악용 중.
- **CVE-2026-20963**: SharePoint, RCE, CVSS 높음, CISA KEV 등록 및 활발히 악용 중.
- **CVE-2026-42897**: Exchange Server, RCE, CVSS 높음, 제로데이로 활발히 악용 중.

### 5. IOC 요약
- **CVE**:
    - CVE-2026-8043 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/05/ivanti-fortinet-sap-vmware-n8n-patch.html))
    - CVE-2025-68670 ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/cve-2025-68670/119742/))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **패치 적용**: `cPanel`, `Exchange Server`, `NGINX` 등 활발히 악용 중인 CVE에 대한 긴급 보안 업데이트 수행.
- **공급망 보안**: 개발자 워크스테이션의 접근 제어 강화 및 NPM 등 외부 의존성 패키지 검증 프로세스 도입.
- **피싱 대응**: 국세청/금융기관 사칭 메일 주의 공지 및 임직원 대상 피싱 모의 훈련 실시.

- **점수**: 8.5/10
